title: Damian Paletta, The Wall Street Journal’s Washington coverage chief, answers reader questions about President Trump and the Iran war. Read more in today’s WSJ Politics Newsletter:
After Cesar Chavez Allegations, California Sex Abuse Laws Could Put U.F.W. at Financial Risk
Oil spikes after Iran says world’s largest gas fields hit in strikes, vows to attack Gulf oil production in retaliation
The Fed left rates unchanged as an oil shock threatens to prolong its inflation fight. Officials held out the prospect of a rate cut this year.
President Trump has spent months talking about how badly he wants Chair Jerome Powell out of the Federal Reserve. Powell signaled Wednesday that he isn’t going anywhere.
Tulsi Gabbard and Top Intelligence Officials Will Testify on Iran War in Senate Hearing
Israel Is Hunting Down Iranian Regime Members in Their Hideouts, One by One
An updated list shows House departures hitting a new high for a midterm-election cycle as members cite a range of political and personal reasons
Bank of Canada holds key interest rate at 2.25%, saying war will boost global inflation
Cesar Chavez accused of abusing girls, raping fellow leader Dolores Huerta, newspaper inquiry finds
